mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in real activity proxies signals near-term revenue growth and improving risk appetite; when RSAFS trends upward, S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive because expected corporate sales revisions dominate discount-rate effects.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of walk-forward observations or Rank IC is below 0.03 for 3 consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% raises the equity discount rate and tightens financial conditions; when FEDFUNDS crosses above this threshold, S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative unless earnings growth offsets valuation compression.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if mean triggered return is not below the unconditional mean at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate at or above 4.5% signals labor-market slack and rising recession risk; when UNRATE is elevated, S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative as investors price weaker future earnings.
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE >= 4.5% is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered instances or the triggered mean return is not lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: High policy rates combined with weak housing starts signal rate-sensitive demand contraction; when FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% and HOUST is below 1250, S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative through lower construction, credit, and durable-goods demand.
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy with housing weakness
failure_condition: Reject if the joint trigger does not underperform either single-series trigger, or if triggered 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of walk-forward cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates stronger goods-sector output and operating leverage; when INDPRO 3-month momentum is positive, S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive because cyclical earnings expectations improve.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO momentum is not associated with higher subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns than negative INDPRO momentum in walk-forward tests, or Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation pressure paired with restrictive rates signals lower valuation multiples and policy persistence; when CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation is rising while FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative.
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction coefficient is not negative in walk-forward regressions or if triggered 20-day returns exceed unconditional returns in more than half of evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth without a simultaneous rise in unemployment signals resilient household income and lower recession risk; when PAYEMS 3-month growth is positive and UNRATE does not rise, S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor-resilience trigger has a nonpositive mean 5-day return or fails to outperform cases with rising UNRATE by at least 10 basis points per event.

mechanism_narrative: Broad demand acceleration across GDP and retail sales signals stronger aggregate earnings; when GDPC1 and RSAFS both have positive recent growth, S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive as revenue revisions outweigh late-cycle margin pressure.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 latest quarterly change > 0 and RSAFS 3-month change > 0
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous GDPC1 and RSAFS growth is followed by below-baseline 60-day S&P 500 returns in most walk-forward windows or if the interaction term is statistically indistinguishable from zero.
